note: News is categorized for StallPlus messaging. Hidden camera and safety stories should be framed as additional privacy protection, not guaranteed prevention.

categoryCounts.label: Viral Social Media About US Bathroom Stalls

articles.title: Heath duo accused of using public bathroom camera to make child porn
articles.description: Columbus man who ran a red light and T-boned an SUV at Sunbury and Morse was later fatally struck; police probe. * A Heath man and woman face charges after police allege they made child pornography. * Police accuse the pair of placing a hidden camera inside a public bathroom in Newark. * Authorities discovered the videos on a laptop and other devices on June 24. A Heath man and woman face criminal charges after prosecutors allege in court documents they were creating child pornography when they put a camera inside a public bathroom in Newark. Brianna N. Nethers, 23, and John M. Nethers, 43, each face one count of pandering sexual material involving a minor, Licking County Common Pleas Court records show. In a criminal complaint, police allege Brianna Nethers put a hidden camera from her purse inside a public bathroom in Newark before bringing a minor to that area to engage in sexual activity. Police allege that John Nethers then uploaded the video to his laptop. Police allege another video shows Brianna Nethers putting the camera on a hidden purse in a public restroom in Licking County, though the complaint doesn't state in which municipality. In the video, several people use the bathroom, including minors, police allege in the complaint Newark police found several devices June 24, including John Nethers' laptop on which they allege they found files containing the videos, according to the complaint. Deputies arrested both of them June 24, and they each had bond hearings June 25, court records show.
articles.stallplusAngle: Hidden camera stories raise restroom privacy expectations. "Heath duo accused of using public bathroom camera to make child porn" supports additional sightline protection messaging without claiming surveillance detection or guaranteed prevention.
